ud6.1:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Vesālī at the Gabled Hall in the Great Forest.
ud6.1:1.3: Then, early in the morning, he adjusted his under robe and–carrying his bowl & robes–went into Vesālī for alms.
ud6.1:1.4: Then, having gone for alms in Vesālī, after the meal, returning from his alms round, he addressed Ven. Ānanda,
ud6.1:1.6: We will go to the Pāvāla shrine for the day’s abiding.”
ud6.1:2.1:
Responding,
“As you say, lord,” to the Blessed One, Ven. Ānanda followed along behind the Blessed One, carrying the sitting cloth.
ud6.1:2.2: Then the Blessed One went to the Pāvāla shrine and, on arrival, sat down on the seat laid out.
ud6.1:2.3: Seated, the Blessed One addressed Ven. Ānanda,
ud6.1:3.1:
“Vesālī is refreshing, Ānanda.
Refreshing, too, are the Udena shrine, the Gotamaka shrine,
the Sattamba shrine, the ManySon shrine, the Sāranda shrine, the Pāvāla shrine.
ud6.1:3.2:
“Anyone, Ānanda, in whom the four bases of power are developed, pursued, given a means of transport, given a grounding,
steadied, consolidated, and well-undertaken, could–if he wanted– remain for an eon or the remainder of an eon.
ud6.1:3.3:
In the Tathāgata, Ānanda, the four bases of power are developed, pursued,
given a means of transport, given a grounding, steadied, consolidated, and well-undertaken.
He could–if he wanted–remain for an eon or the remainder of an eon.”
ud6.1:4.1:
But Ven. Ānanda–even when the Blessed One had given such a blatant sign,
such a blatant hint–wasn’t able to understand his meaning.
ud6.1:4.3:
“Lord, may the Blessed One remain for an eon.
May the One-Well-Gone remain for an eon–for the benefit of the many, for the happiness of the many,
out of sympathy for the world, for the welfare, benefit, & happiness of human and divine beings.”
It was as if his mind was possessed by Māra.
ud6.1:4.5: A third time, the Blessed One addressed Ven. Ānanda,
ud6.1:5.1:
“Vesālī is refreshing, Ānanda.
Refreshing, too, are the Udena shrine, the Gotamaka shrine,
the Sattamba shrine, the ManySon shrine, the Sāranda shrine, the Pāvāla shrine.
ud6.1:5.2:
“Anyone, Ānanda, in whom the four bases of power are developed, pursued, given a means of transport, given a grounding,
steadied, consolidated, and well-undertaken, could–if he wanted–remain for an eon or the remainder of an eon.
ud6.1:5.3:
In the Tathāgata, Ānanda, the four bases of power are developed, pursued,
given a means of transport, given a grounding, steadied, consolidated, and well-undertaken.
He could–if he wanted–remain for an eon or the remainder of an eon.”
ud6.1:6.1:
But Ven. Ānanda–even when the Blessed One had given such a blatant sign,
such a blatant hint–wasn’t able to understand his meaning.
ud6.1:6.3:
“Lord, may the Blessed One remain for an eon.
May the One-Well-Gone remain for an eon–for the benefit of the many, for the happiness of the many,
out of sympathy for the world, for the welfare, benefit, & happiness of human and divine beings.”
It was as if his mind was possessed by Māra.
ud6.1:7.1: Then the Blessed One addressed Ven. Ānanda,
ud6.1:7.2:
“Go, Ānanda.
Do what you think it is now time to do.”
ud6.1:7.3:
Responding,
“As you say, lord,” to the Blessed One, Ven. Ānanda got up from his seat, bowed down to the Blessed One and–
after circling him to the right–went to sit under a tree not far from the Blessed One.
ud6.1:8.1:
Then, not long after Ven. Ānanda had left, Māra the Evil One went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, stood to one side.
As he was standing there he said to the Blessed One,
ud6.1:9.1:
“May the Blessed One totally unbind now, lord.
May the One-Well-Gone totally unbind now, lord.
Now is the time for the Blessed One’s total unbinding, lord.
ud6.1:9.2: After all, these words were said by the Blessed One:
ud6.1:9.5:
“May the Blessed One totally unbind now, lord.
May the One-Well-Gone totally unbind now, lord.
Now is the time for the Blessed One’s total unbinding, lord.
ud6.1:10.1:
After all, these words were said by the Blessed One:
‘Evil One, I will not totally unbind as long as my nun disciples… my male lay-follower disciples…
ud6.1:12.4:
“May the Blessed One totally unbind now, lord.
May the One-Well-Gone totally unbind now, lord.
Now is the time for the Blessed One’s total unbinding, lord.
ud6.1:13.1: After all, these words were said by the Blessed One:
ud6.1:13.2:
‘Evil One, I will not totally unbind as long as this holy life of mine is not powerful, prosperous,
widely-spread, disseminated among many people, well-expounded as far as there are devas & human beings.’
ud6.1:13.3:
But now, lord, the Blessed One’s holy life is powerful, prosperous, widely-spread,
disseminated among many people, well-expounded as far as there are devas & human beings.
ud6.1:13.4:
“May the Blessed One totally unbind now, lord.
May the One-Well-Gone totally unbind now, lord.
Now is the time for the Blessed One’s total unbinding, lord.”
ud6.1:14.1: When this was said, the Blessed One said to Māra, the Most Evil One:
ud6.1:14.2:
“Relax, Evil One.
It won’t be long until the Tathāgata’s total unbinding.
ud6.1:14.3: In three months’ time from now, the Tathāgata will totally unbind.”
ud6.1:15.1: Thus at the Pāvāla shrine–mindful & alert–the Blessed One relinquished the fabrications of life.
ud6.1:15.2:
And as the Blessed One relinquished the fabrications of life,
there was a great earthquake, frightening & hair-raising, along with cracks of thunder.
ud6.1:16.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud6.1:17.2: the sage relinquished the fabrication of becoming.
ud6.1:17.4: he split his own coming-into-being like a coat of mail.
